content:
The core innovation isn’t technological (audiences have voted on performance since John Cage and Alvin Lucier). The innovation is architectural: treating different performance genres—music, dance, theater, visual art—as interchangeable applications sharing a unified audience-input computational infrastructure. No single existing platform does this across genres with validated real-time performance.

WHY YOU, AND WHY NOW

I’ve watched you hold narrative coherence while being the connective tissue between disparate theatrical pieces. You understand how structure is meaning. You ask the right questions when something doesn’t work. You know the difference between constraint that generates depth and constraint that suffocates.

Those same principles—precision, pacing, strategic management of revelation—are exactly what make this system work dramatically rather than devolve into chaos.

And crucially: you’re heading to the Carolinas to do creative work. This is the moment when you’re most generative, most willing to think sideways about problems. I want to introduce this while you’re in that headspace.
